edibility: Edible only when interior is pure white (very young stage); generally listed as inedible due to bitter, dark mature spores; lycoperdonosis (respiratory inflammation) risk if mature spores are inhaled in significant quantities.
taste: Mild and bland when very young and flesh is still white; bitter and unpleasant when older as flesh darkens and spores mature.
search: Found in late summer through autumn in acidic woodlands, heaths, and grasslands. Look for the distinctive dark/dusky spines covering the globe-shaped fruiting body. Similar Lycoperdon species may be confused with it, so check spore color and spine characteristics.

toxicity.symptoms: Lycoperdonosis (lung inflammation/bronchitis) if mature spores inhaled in quantity; gastrointestinal distress (nausea, vomiting) if mature flesh ingested.
toxicity.antidote: Corticosteroids if respiratory involvement; supportive care and monitoring for gastrointestinal cases
